title: Abdominal Distension - Decompensated Cirrhosis
chief_complaint: Progressive abdominal distension over 2 weeks, yellow discoloration of eyes, and easy bruising. Known alcoholic liver disease.

physical_exam.abdomen: Distended, shifting dullness, fluid wave positive

hidden_data.us_abd: Nodular liver, ascites, splenomegaly, no focal lesions

patient_responses.alcohol: I've been drinking heavily for 25 years
patient_responses.abdominal_swelling: My belly has been getting bigger for 2 weeks

hints.complications: Always rule out SBP with paracentesis

feedback.correct_message: Excellent diagnosis, Doctor. Decompensated cirrhosis with ascites is correct.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is decompensated cirrhosis. The SAAG >1.1 confirms portal hypertension.

ascitic_fluid.comment: Diagnostic paracentesis - SAAG >1.1 confirms portal hypertension.

feedback.attending_note: Decompensated cirrhosis requires comprehensive management: sodium restriction, diuretics (spironolactone + furosemide), SBP prophylaxis, HCC screening, and transplant evaluation. Well done.
